problem: The hlx-cli agent skill (SKILL.md + references/) lives outside the CLI package today. Users and agents must manually locate and copy skill files. This ticket bundles the canonical skill content inside the published @projectxinnovation/helix-cli npm package and adds a `hlx skill` command with `show` (print to stdout) and `install` (copy to agent skills directory) subcommands.

files.why: CLI entry point and top-level command router. The new `skill` case must be added to the switch statement (line 69-117). Commands without auth (login, token, update) are dispatched without configOrHelp(). SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set on line 61 may need the 'skill' command added.
Controls npm publish payload via `files` field (currently only includes `dist`). Must be updated to include bundled skill content directory. Also defines build/test scripts and version (1.3.2).
TypeScript compiler config. rootDir=src, outDir=dist. tsc does NOT copy non-TS files, so static skill files cannot live in src/ and be compiled to dist/. Determines how runtime path resolution works.
Controls npm tarball exclusions. Currently excludes test files only. Must not exclude bundled skill files.
CI pipeline validates tarball contents before npm publish. Currently checks for dist/index.js and package.json presence, and rejects test files. May need skill file validation added.
Shared flag parsing: getFlag(), hasFlag(), isHelpRequested(), requireFlag(), getPositionalArgs(). The skill command handlers will use these utilities for --target, --for, --force, --help.
Reference command module showing the standard subcommand router pattern: usage function, switch dispatch, per-subcommand help, isHelpRequested() checks.
Reference for a no-auth command pattern. Dispatched directly from index.ts without configOrHelp(). The skill command follows this same no-auth pattern.
Shows runtime path resolution pattern using import.meta.url and dirname(fileURLToPath()). The skill command needs similar resolution to locate bundled skill files relative to the installed package.
Shows how to resolve the global npm install path and validate package integrity. Relevant to the corrupt-install detection requirement for skill commands.
Uses homedir() from node:os for ~/.hlx/ path. The skill install command needs homedir() to resolve ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.codex/skills/.
Test pattern reference: uses node:test built-in runner with describe/it blocks and node:assert. New skill tests should follow this pattern.
Additional test pattern reference showing beforeEach/afterEach for env var manipulation. Relevant for skill install tests that mock filesystem state.

facts.claim: The CLI entry point uses a switch statement in src/index.ts (lines 69-117) to route top-level commands. New commands are added as new case blocks.
Commands that don't need authentication (login, token, update) are dispatched without configOrHelp(). The skill command falls into this category.
The SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set contains --version, -v, update, --help, -h. Commands in this set bypass the auto-update check.
facts.evidence: src/index.ts line 61: const SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE = new Set(["--version", "-v", "update", "--help", "-h"])

facts.claim: The files field in package.json only includes dist/ (minus test files). Static non-JS files placed anywhere other than dist/ are not included in the npm tarball.
tsc compiles src/ to dist/ but does NOT copy non-TypeScript files. SKILL.md and references/*.md cannot be placed in src/ and expected to appear in dist/.
facts.evidence: tsconfig.json: rootDir=src, outDir=dist. TypeScript compiler only emits .js and .d.ts files.

facts.claim: Runtime path resolution to package root uses dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) and navigates up. From dist/update/version.js, package.json is at ../../package.json.
No SKILL.md or references/ directory currently exists anywhere in the helix-cli repository.
facts.evidence: Glob search for **/SKILL.md and **/references/** in the repo returned no results.

facts.claim: The canonical skill format (as used by Claude agent) is a directory with SKILL.md at root and optional references/ subdirectory with markdown files.
facts.evidence: Workspace .claude/skills/ directory structure: each skill is <name>/SKILL.md with optional <name>/references/*.md

facts.claim: Target install directories are ~/.claude/skills/<name>/ and ~/.codex/skills/<name>/. Neither exists in the current sandbox environment.
facts.evidence: Bash check: ls ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.codex/skills/ both returned 'not found'

facts.claim: Tests use Node.js built-in test runner (node:test). No external test frameworks. Run via: npm test which compiles then runs node --test dist/**/*.test.js
facts.evidence: package.json scripts.test: "tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js"; src/lib/flags.test.ts imports from node:test

facts.claim: The CI publish workflow packs the tarball and validates contents before publishing. It checks for dist/index.js and package.json presence, and rejects .test.js files.
The codebase uses only Node.js built-in modules (node:fs, node:path, node:os, node:http, node:crypto, node:child_process). No runtime dependencies.
facts.evidence: package.json has zero dependencies (only devDependencies: @types/node, typescript)

facts.claim: The package is ESM (type: module) targeting ES2022 with Node16 module resolution.
facts.evidence: package.json: "type": "module"; tsconfig.json: target ES2022, module Node16

facts.claim: The usage() function in src/index.ts (lines 33-58) serves as the global help screen. New commands should be listed here.
Subcommand modules export a single router function (runInspect, runComments, runTickets, etc.) that takes args and optionally config.
facts.evidence: src/inspect/index.ts exports runInspect(config, args); src/comments/index.ts exports runComments(config, args); src/token/index.ts exports runToken(args)

unknowns.question: What is the canonical SKILL.md content for the hlx-cli skill? The ticket says to bundle 'verbatim copies' but the content does not exist in the repository yet.
unknowns.impact: Implementation must either author the skill content or locate the existing canonical source. The ticket says it 'lives outside the CLI today' but does not specify where.

unknowns.question: Which specific files should be in the references/ directory? The ticket mentions 'references/*' but no canonical source is identified.
unknowns.impact: Determines the set of files to bundle and copy during install.

unknowns.question: Should the bundled skill files live in a top-level directory (e.g., skill-content/) added to files[], or should a build step copy them into dist/?
unknowns.impact: Affects package.json files field, build pipeline, and runtime path resolution logic.

unknowns.question: Should 'skill' be added to SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE? It's a local-only command that doesn't need network access.
unknowns.impact: Minor UX consideration — auto-update check before a purely local command adds latency.
